notes: In connection with our spiritual powers of evaluation, we need to remember that the Spirit of the Lord will not guide us if our own attitude is one of faultfinding. That principle applies to readers and writers. The scriptures abound with the commandment that Christians should abstain from evil speaking (see Ephesians 4:31; 1 Peter 2:1; D&C 20:54; 136:23). We should stress the positive and seek to strengthen one another in all our communications (see D&C 108:7). President Gordon B. Hinckley gave valuable counsel on that subject in a general conference address several years ago: "We live in a society that feeds on criticism. Faultfinding is the substance of columnists and commentators, and there is too much of this among our own people. It is so easy to find fault, and to
wherefore then hast thou not kept thy lord the king? for there came one of the people in to destroy the king thy lord.
